free_text: Excitation functions for radioactive isotopes produced
by protons below 60 MeV on Al, Fe, and Cu
Metallic foils of natural element,
thickness ranging from 0.005 to 0.02 inch.
For all foils irradiated no activities due to
impurities were noted.
proton energy in each foil was calculated according
to H.Bichsel, tech.Rept.No.3,Physics department,
university of southern California, 1961(unpublished).
Energy uncertainty for the data ranges from +-0.35 to
+-0.55 MeV including the energy spread of the incident
beam, and the energy-loss straggling.
a probable error of 30% of the cross
sections was stated without further specification.
For all irradiations the accuracy of beam current
integration was 1%.
Contribution by reactions induced by secondary neutrons
could be neglected as experimentally shown.
LAPF
Some corrections have been included by
CAJAD
Some reaction lines is corrected. Lower
case.
Last checking has been done.
VS. DATA-ERR and COMMON added.
Reaction corrected in 020.
DECAY-DATA corrected in 002.
the abundances given are gamma transitions per decay
not taking into account internal conversion.
By KACHAPAG.
The cross sections given are taken from the published
smooth curve at arbitrary energy values (comment by
the compiler).
